title: silene nutans subsp. livida
intro: Silene nutans subsp. livida, also known as the Alpine Catchfly, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Caryophyllaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been used to treat various health issues. The plant has been traditionally used to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and treat digestive issues, with its health benefits attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Herbal preparations of Silene nutans subsp. livida include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
